mission.title: Install and Configure Inspektor Gadget on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inspektor Gadget is a set of tools and framework for data collection and system inspection on Kubernetes clusters and Linux hosts using eBPF. Installing it allows you to leverage eBPF for enhanced observability and monitoring of your applications and infrastructure.

steps.title: Add the Inspektor Gadget Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for Inspektor Gadget to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Inspektor Gadget using Helm
steps.description:
Install Inspektor Gadget in the `inspektor-gadget` namespace. This command will also create the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Inspektor Gadget pods are running successfully in the `inspektor-gadget` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Inspektor Gadget deployment. Edit the deployment to set resource limits as needed.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

steps.title: Access the Inspektor Gadget dashboard
steps.description:
If there is a dashboard or service to access, set up port forwarding to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Inspektor Gadget pods in the `Running` state when you run `kubectl get pods`. You can also access the dashboard through the port-forwarding command provided.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Inspektor Gadget release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources created by the installation.
```bash
helm uninstall inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources related to Inspektor Gadget are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd gadgets.inspektor-gadget.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `inspektor-gadget` namespace to remove any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been deleted successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace inspektor-gadget
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of your Inspektor Gadget installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace inspektor-gadget -o yaml > inspektor-gadget-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Inspektor Gadget release to the latest version. Make sure to specify the desired version if needed.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget --version v0.49.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Inspektor Gadget pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Inspektor Gadget pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the pods to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Inspektor Gadget dashboard, ensure that the service is running and check the port forwarding command. Verify the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits defined in the deployment. You may need to adjust them or allocate more resources to your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to CRDs, ensure that the CRDs were installed correctly. You can list the CRDs to verify their presence.
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep gadgets
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
